insight: Strong non-neural floor; retrieving real human answers scores well on ROUGE due to fluent reference-like text. Establishes that retrieval beats generation for this task.

key_config: fp32, Adafactor, grad checkpointing, 3 epochs, beam=4, language-aware prefix prompts
insight: Generation significantly underperforms retrieval on ROUGE. Model rephrases rather than reproducing reference wording. mT5 has limited coverage of Akan and Luganda in its pretraining data.

key_config: n-gram sweep over (2,4)/(2,5)/(3,5)/(3,6)/(4,6); word+char combo tested
insight: Shorter char n-grams (2,4) generalize better across morphological variation in agglutinative languages like Akan and Luganda. Word-level features and top-k-longest selection both hurt. Local ROUGE proxy validated against leaderboard.
Adding word-level features did NOT improve over char-only. Morphological complexity of African languages means word boundaries are less informative than character patterns.

key_config: fp32 Adafactor grad-checkpoint, 5 epochs converged, beam=4 len_penalty=1.2 min_tokens=15
insight: AfriTeVa beats mT5 by +0.057 as predicted by literature — Africa-centric pretraining helps. Tokenizer UNK rate: Akan 2.0/1000 vs mT5 8.4/1000. Still below retrieval: generation rephrases rather than reproducing reference wording.

key_config: IndexFlatIP, 36501 vectors, mean-pool + L2-norm, per-language + cross-lingual top-K
insight: Semantic embeddings capture topic-level similarity that TF-IDF character n-grams miss. +0.078 over best TF-IDF. Enables cross-lingual retrieval (81% of test entries need it). Index built from train+val combined.

key_config: temp=0.1, max_tokens=600, top-5 same-lang + top-3 cross-lang context, prompt: maximize word overlap
insight: Prompt engineered to reuse exact phrasing from retrieved context. Low temperature for deterministic verbatim reproduction. Same-language contexts prioritized in prompt ordering.

key_config: temp=0.3, max_tokens=600, emphasis on factual accuracy + completeness + language quality
insight: Separate generation strategy for LLM-as-Judge column. Higher temperature for more natural writing. Prompt emphasizes factual accuracy and cultural appropriateness over lexical overlap.
Exploits the 3-column submission format: each column evaluated by a different metric. ROUGE columns get retrieval-augmented verbatim answers; LLM column gets quality-focused generation.

model: Best of retrieval vs generation per-entry
key_config: Score-based selection: use retrieval when high similarity score, generation otherwise
insight: Final ensemble combines retrieval confidence scores with generation quality. High-confidence retrievals likely outperform generation on ROUGE; generation better for novel questions.
